care.summary: Compact citrus tree with small, oval orange fruits. Sweet edible skin with tart flesh. Excellent container plant that can fruit indoors with good light.
care.light: Full sun — at least 6 hours daily for best fruiting; tolerates bright indirect indoors
care.water: Regular watering; keep soil moist but not soggy. Allow top inch to dry between waterings
care.soil: Well-draining citrus or cactus mix; slightly acidic (pH 5.5-6.5)
care.humidity: Moderate to high; mist leaves in dry indoor environments
care.fertilizer: Citrus-specific fertilizer every 4-6 weeks during growing season; reduce in winter
care.toxicity: Fruit is edible; leaves and rind safe. Avoid consuming ornamental varieties treated with pesticides
common_issues: Aphids and scale insects on new growth
Yellowing leaves from overwatering or nutrient deficiency
Poor fruit set in low-light indoor conditions
Root rot from cold, wet soil
Spider mites in dry indoor air
tips: Cold-hardy for citrus — tolerates brief dips to -5°C
Eat whole: the sweet skin is the best part, flesh is tart
Great for container growing on sunny patios or indoors
Hand-pollinate indoors with a small brush for fruit set
Thin excess fruit to encourage larger kumquats
